skill.name: industry-key-contact-radar-api-skill
skill.description: Discover public key contacts for a target industry, website, and job title by running BrowserAct's Industry Key Contact Radar API workflow.
skill.summary: Find public industry contacts through the BrowserAct contact discovery workflow.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The critical static heuristic is dismissed because no obfuscation or arbitrary code execution was found. The skill does run a local Python script that sends user parameters and a BrowserAct API key to an external API, then polls for results. Publishing is acceptable with a clear warning about credential handling, external network use, and public-profile data compliance.

medium_findings.title: BrowserAct API Key Handling Requires Care
medium_findings.description: The skill requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment and also tells users they may provide the key in chat. This is legitimate API authentication, but it can expose a secret if users paste credentials into conversation history.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable and user-facing credential guidance are explicit. The risk is credential exposure, not evidence of malicious theft.

medium_findings.title: External API Requests Send User Search Parameters
medium_findings.description: The script posts the industry, result limit, target site, and job title to BrowserAct, then polls BrowserAct for task status and output. This is expected for the skill, but users should know their search criteria are sent to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The requests.post and requests.get calls directly target the BrowserAct workflow API. The payload contains user-controlled search parameters.

medium_findings.title: Automated Public Profile Collection Has Compliance Risk
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to collect public profile URLs, names, introductions, and company associations for lead generation and sales prospecting. This may be acceptable, but users must follow platform terms, privacy rules, and consent requirements.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The intended output fields and use cases are clearly documented. The severity depends on user jurisdiction, target platform rules, and downstream use.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Many static external-command findings in SKILL.md are markdown inline code or a fenced example. The actual command is a documented Python invocation and does not use shell interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows markdown examples and parameter names, not Ruby backtick execution. The command calls a local Python script with quoted arguments.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded BrowserAct URLs Are Expected
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the BrowserAct API and BrowserAct account pages. They are expected service endpoints and are not suspicious by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs align with the skill's stated BrowserAct integration. No unrelated destination or hidden exfiltration endpoint was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited SKILL.md lines do not implement cryptography or select a cryptographic algorithm. No evidence of MD5, SHA1, or another weak cryptographic primitive was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the flagged lines found product description text only. The Python file also does not import or call cryptographic libraries.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Credential Used In Outbound Authorization Header
dangerous_patterns.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and places it into a Bearer Authorization header for BrowserAct requests. This is normal API use, but it is a sensitive credential flow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential source and Authorization header construction are explicit. The endpoint is the documented BrowserAct API, so this is sensitive but not malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Long-Running External Workflow Polling
dangerous_patterns.description: The script can start a BrowserAct workflow and poll for up to 900 seconds. This may consume account quota and should be run only with explicit user intent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow start and polling loop are clear in the code. The risk is resource use and third-party automation, not hidden execution.

content.value_statement: Finding relevant decision-makers across public platforms is slow and inconsistent. This skill runs BrowserAct contact discovery and returns profile links, names, bios, and companies.
actual_capabilities: Runs the BrowserAct Industry Key Contact Radar workflow through a Python script.
Accepts industry, result limit, target site, and job title parameters.
Reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the local environment.
Starts a BrowserAct workflow task and polls until completion or timeout.
Prints profile URLs, names, introductions, and company associations from returned results.
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key and active account quota.
Sends search parameters to the BrowserAct API.
Results depend on BrowserAct workflow availability and target site data.
Does not verify consent, platform terms, or outreach compliance.

use_cases.title: Build Founder Prospect Lists
use_cases.description: Find public founder profiles in a target industry and export concise contact context.

use_cases.title: Map Competitive Leadership
use_cases.description: Identify visible executives or managers associated with competing companies and markets.

use_cases.title: Source Recruiting Targets
use_cases.description: Search public platforms for people with specific roles in a selected industry.

prompt_templates.title: Find Basic Contacts
prompt_templates.prompt: Find up to 10 founder profiles for the Browser automation industry on facebook.com.

prompt_templates.title: Target a Site and Role
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused search for one platform and job title.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the contact radar to find 15 marketing manager profiles in healthcare on linkedin.com.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Candidate Segments
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate research across roles or platforms.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for growth leaders in e-commerce on github.com, then summarize useful profile patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Compliance-Aware Outreach Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Find public CEO profiles in fintech, list the source platform, and flag any records that need manual compliance review.

output_examples.input: Find five founders in browser automation on facebook.com.
output: A short list of public profile links matching the industry and founder role.
Each item includes the visible name, profile introduction, and associated company when returned.

output_examples.input: Search for marketing managers in healthcare on linkedin.com.
output: A role-focused contact list from the selected site.
The response highlights names, profile URLs, brief bios, and organization details.

best_practices: Store BROWSERACT_API_KEY as an environment variable instead of pasting it into chat.
Use narrow industry, site, and role parameters to reduce irrelevant results.
Review platform terms and privacy obligations before using results for outreach.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to collect private or restricted account information.
Do not run broad searches that may waste BrowserAct quota.
Do not treat returned profiles as verified leads without manual review.

faq.question: What does this skill connect to?
faq.answer: It connects to the BrowserAct workflow API using a local Python script.

faq.question: Does it need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY in the local environment.

faq.question: What inputs can I control?
faq.answer: You can set the industry, maximum results, target site, and job title.

faq.question: What results does it return?
faq.answer: It can return public profile URLs, names, introductions, and company associations.

faq.question: Can it verify contact accuracy?
faq.answer: No. You should manually review important records before using them.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is medium risk and publishable with warnings about credentials, network access, and compliance.
